etymology.description: semantic 囗(囗) (wéi, "fence") + phonetic 袁(袁) (yuán) -> garden enclosed by a fence
etymology.interpretation: The character 囗(囗) (wéi, "fence") suggests an enclosed area, a common feature of gardens. 袁(袁) (yuán) provides the sound. Together, they form the concept of a garden, which is typically a fenced or confined area designated for cultivation or enjoyment.
etymology.simplification: 园(園): Simplified by substituting 袁(袁) (yuán) with the more straightforward character 元(元) (yuán, "origin"), reducing stroke complexity while keeping the phonetic element intact.

examples.chinese: 这个花园里有很多种花(這個花園裡有很多種花)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège huāyuán lǐ yǒu hěn duō zhǒng huā.
examples.english: There are many kinds of flowers in this garden.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢在园子里种花(她喜歡在園子裡種花)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài yuánzi lǐ zhòng huā.
examples.english: She likes to plant flowers in the garden.

examples.chinese: 我们周末常去那座公园(我們周末常去那座公園)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhōumò cháng qù nà zuò gōngyuán.
examples.english: We often go to that park on weekends.
